subject: [POLICY] Increase hikari connection pool size for PAP & API
commit_message:
[POLICY] Increase hikari connection pool size for PAP & API

We saw some bottlenecks during S3P testing of API component due
to the default connection pool size of 10. Setting it to 20 based
on the number of parallel threads used for performance tests.
The change is required because prior to spring boot migration
(Istanbul and earlier), the default connection size was 32 so
it was never a problem for running load tests.

Issue-ID: POLICY-4007
Change-Id: Iaaa573d5fce0298e3aff05a27c2e845840a6cb06
Signed-off-by: Prakhar Pandey <prakhar.pandey@bell.ca>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 13 on the queue.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

I think it is also worth mentioning that, until last release, connection pool size wasn't specified and the default value(32 I think) was getting used. In spring boot, the default value is 10, which resulted in bottlenecks while running API performance test using 20 threads.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 13 on the queue.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> I think it is also worth mentioning that, until last release, connection pool size wasn't specified and the default value(32 I think) was getting used. In spring boot, the default value is 10, which resulted in bottlenecks while running API performance test using 20 threads.

Updated commit message - I guess you will have to re-approve.
